anomalies: pokemon-crystal:trades:dragonair-dodrio: source disagreement on the Dodrio's held item — Serebii (src-serebii-trades) lists a Bitter Berry, Bulbapedia (src-bulba-locations) lists a Smoke Ball. Flagged for review; Serebii used as primary per Pierre's direction.

Trades sourced from the Serebii Crystal in-game trades page (src-serebii-trades, fetched 2026-06-18 at Pierre's direction; snapshot saved at pokemon-crystal/sources/serebii-crystal-trades.md). Serebii's Crystal list contains exactly these 7 trades; the Vermilion Farfetch'd-for-Spearow trade (Bulbapedia, tagged R/B/Y and FR/LG) is not a Crystal trade and is excluded.

notes: Fills deck slot 6 (flowchart, global, defaultTitle "In-game Trades"). 7 nodes, one per Crystal in-game NPC trade (give → receive), each checkable. No edges — in-game trades are independent, not a chained flow.

Trades: Violet (Bellsprout→Onix/Kyle), Goldenrod (Abra→Machop/Mike), Olivine (Krabby→Voltorb/Tim), Blackthorn (Dragonair→Dodrio/Emy), Pewter (Haunter→Xatu/Chris), Power Plant (Dugtrio→Magneton/Forest), Route 14 (Chansey→Aerodactyl/Kim). NPC names, nicknames, and held items reproduced from Serebii; the three Johto trades also corroborated by the Bulbapedia city pages (src-bulba-locations).

Per Pierre (2026-06-18), this slot is filled directly from source rather than from a data.json trades dataset; src-serebii-trades was appended to sources.json (append-only) and a snapshot saved. If a trades dataset is later added in extract-data, re-run this widget to consume it (keeping these node IDs).
